name: HubSpot

evidence.claim: HubSpot APIs are built using REST conventions and accessible via api.hubapi.com.
Developers can access the API by signing up for a free HubSpot account and using the Developer Dashboard.
Search citation: Ask HN: Can someone at HubSpot explain some of their API design choices? | Hacker News
Search citation: HubSpot API Explained: Automate CRM Tasks Like a Pro

access_notes: Developers can sign up for a free HubSpot account to access the developer dashboard and API features.
auth_notes: HubSpot officially pushes OAuth2 for integrations. While older documentation and legacy systems mentioned API keys, these are being deprecated or discouraged in favor of more secure authentication methods like OAuth2.
mcp_notes: No official or prominent community-maintained Model Context Protocol (MCP) server was identified in the provided sources.
one_liner: HubSpot is a CRM and growth platform that provides a broad REST API for managing marketing, sales, and customer service data.
api_notes: HubSpot provides a extensive REST API that covers CRM objects (contacts, deals, companies), marketing, service, and other platform features. Legacy APIs are being transitioned to versioned, RESTful endpoints.
